prompt_queue.prompt: Run the PR-first iterator against the fetched finding packet item supplied by the fetch step. The transformer primary workspaces are preloaded by the bundle; do not call workspace_clone for static-site-importer, html-to-blocks-converter, or block-format-bridge. First classify the root owner repo: DOM-to-block output signals such as core/html, core/freeform, fallback block output, unsupported wrapper conversion, and invalid block serialization usually belong to html-to-blocks-converter, not static-site-importer. Use static-site-importer only for document selection, region/report generation, asset/theme writing, or validation artifact behavior. Before creating a worktree, inspect the preloaded primary handle with read-only tools; prefer workspace_grep to find exact architecture anchors and nearby smoke tests, then workspace_read the matched path/offset. Call workspace_worktree_add only after you have a grounded patch path and exact source anchor. Open a focused upstream PR with regression coverage when a safe generalized patch path exists; use a fallback issue when evidence cannot support a grounded patch. Workspace preparation is not completion: after successful workspace_worktree_add, use at most four inspection tools including workspace_grep to identify the nearest existing transform family and regression-test style, then the next tool must be workspace_edit or create_github_issue. For html-to-blocks-converter transform behavior, grep/read includes/class-transform-registry.php and tests/smoke-*.php before raw-handler pipeline mechanics. Do not invent files, methods, or classes. Never add standalone global helper functions to plugin bootstrap files as transformer fixes. If two workspace_edit attempts fail with old_string not found, or if no exact architecture anchor is found, stop the PR path and open a focused issue. Continue through status, commit, push, upstream PR or fallback issue, and source PR callback. Do not hardcode generated fixture class names or artifact-specific selectors into runtime code unless they already represent a reusable semantic contract in the target repository.

Before opening a fallback issue, call list_github_issues in the owner repository and check for an existing open issue covering the same source PR, validation run, site slug, signal kind, artifact names, or visual parity mismatch. If a matching issue exists, do not create a duplicate; comment back to the source PR with the existing issue URL and evidence summary. Only call create_github_issue when no existing issue covers the finding.

Treat fallback issues as durable per source finding, not per iterator run. A new validation_run_id or newly worded title is not enough to justify a new issue. When list_github_issues returns an open issue with the same source_pr plus the same site slug and signal family, reuse it. For visual parity packets, match any open visual-parity issue for the same source_pr and site slug. For ignored_region or intentionally ignored accessibility chrome, match any open validation or triage issue for the same source_pr/site slug mentioning ignored_region, intentionally ignored, accessibility chrome, or skip link. When reusing, call comment_github_pull_request with the existing issue URL and complete via existing_issue_path.

Keep transformer fixes bounded by the fetched packet evidence. When a patch generalizes from an observed attribute, match only the observed inert shape unless existing repository tests or source evidence prove a broader shape is safe. Add a negative regression for the nearest functional case that must remain a fallback; for example, if the packet shows a form with action="#", do not also match missing or empty action unless that case is explicitly proven inert.
